error.signature: AI tells a traveler that Kenya is visa-free or offers a visa on arrival

verdict.summary: Since January 2024 Kenya abolished tourist visas and requires nearly ALL visitors - including previously visa-free nationals - to obtain an Electronic Travel Authorisation (eTA) online before travel. Apply at least 3 days ahead on the official eTA portal; airlines check it before boarding. Limited exemptions exist (e.g., East African Community citizens).

dead_ends.action: Assume Kenya is still visa-free for your nationality or sells a visa on arrival
dead_ends.why_fails: Kenya replaced its visa system with a mandatory eTA for nearly all visitors in 2024; even previously visa-free travelers must now apply online in advance, and there is no airport visa counter.
dead_ends.common_misconception: The 2024 reform removed visa-free entry for most nationalities, replacing it with the eTA.

dead_ends.action: Apply through a third-party 'Kenya visa/eTA' website
dead_ends.why_fails: Only the official eTA portal issues a valid authorisation; copycat sites overcharge and may submit incorrectly.

workarounds.action: Apply for the eTA on the official Kenyan government portal at least 3 days before travel.
workarounds.how: Use only etakenya.go.ke; upload your details and passport, pay the fee, and carry the approved eTA.

workarounds.action: Check the limited exemptions (e.g., EAC partner-state citizens, certain transit) on the official site.
workarounds.how: Confirm whether you qualify for an exemption before applying.
